prompt_queue.prompt: Bootstrap the repository's technical, developer-facing living documentation from source code. This is a first-run documentation bootstrap, not a maintenance check. First build a coverage map from README.md, docs/**, source directories, tests, examples, issues, and pull requests. Documentation is not complete until every major public API, extension point, runtime contract, workflow/lifecycle path, data contract, storage/persistence boundary, auth/permission behavior, failure mode, operational workflow, and contributor-facing design principle is documented or explicitly marked out of scope. The default bootstrap output is a documentation pull request that creates or updates a docs index plus module/topic docs and includes the coverage map in the committed documentation. Prefer one coherent docs surface with an index or clear navigation over isolated pages. You must use create_or_update_github_file before create_github_pull_request unless the repository already contains an explicit, current coverage map proving every major source area is documented. Do not stop after a single high-signal gap unless tool, token, or writable-scope limits prevent a full pass; if constrained, state the partial coverage in the committed docs and pull request body. Return no_changes only when existing docs already include a complete, navigable technical docs surface and an explicit coverage map across the full codebase.
